claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) is produced by a label-only encoding where each token is drawn from a small closed vocabulary of fewer than 50 distinct labels, rather than running prose
evidence_for: Zodiac section has only 1,297 words across 12 folios â€” far lower word density than herbal (84 words/folio) or recipes (464 words/folio)
Entropy 3.7149 bits is 0.19 bits below the next-lowest section (astronomical), consistent with a smaller effective alphabet
High-confidence belief (0.243) that zodiac uses homophonic label encoding
Short repeated tokens like 'ol', 'ar', 'or' dominate top-10 word list, plausibly functioning as ordinal or directional labels
evidence_against: If the zodiac vocabulary size is not demonstrably smaller than other sections, the structural explanation loses force
Low entropy could alternatively reflect heavy repetition of a small set of cipher words rather than a label structure
test: Extract the zodiac sub-corpus; compute its unique word count, type-token ratio, and frequency distribution; compare vocabulary coverage curves (what fraction of tokens are covered by the top-N types) against herbal and recipes sections
test_metric: Vocabulary size of zodiac vs. other sections; percentage of zodiac tokens covered by top 20 word types
test_threshold: Confirmation if top 20 word types cover â‰¥ 70% of zodiac tokens and unique zodiac vocabulary â‰¤ 60 types; denial if vocabulary size exceeds 150 or top-20 coverage is below 50%

reasoning: Identifying the zodiac as a closed-label section would isolate it as a potential Rosetta-style entry point where glyph-to-label mappings could be tested against known zodiac names
